data.title: Nippon Coke & Engineering Tightens Net Asset Covenants on Syndicated Loans
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Nippon Coke & Engineering Company, Limited ( (JP:3315) ) has provided an update.

Nippon Coke & Engineering has amended the financial covenants on multiple syndicated loan agreements used to fund capital expenditures and working capital, aligning them with recent changes to its commitment line. The revised terms reset the net asset maintenance benchmark to 80% of net assets as of the fiscal year ended March 2026, replacing the previous standard that referenced earlier fiscal years while retaining the condition that operating income must not be negative for two consecutive years.

The adjustments apply to a 2022 committed term loan and three tranches of a 2024 syndicated facility, together totaling nearly ¥38.7 billion in commitments, and are expected to influence the firm’s longer-term balance sheet management and capital policy. The company said the financial impact of these covenant changes will be reflected in its consolidated forecast for the year ending March 2027, to be disclosed alongside its March 2026 results in mid-May, signaling that lenders and investors should watch for updated guidance on earnings and leverage metrics.

More about Nippon Coke & Engineering Company, Limited

Nippon Coke & Engineering Co., Ltd., listed on the TSE Prime Market under securities code 3315, operates in the coke and engineering sector and relies on sizable syndicated loans to fund capital expenditures and working capital. The company borrows on an unsecured basis from consortia of major city and regional banks, reflecting a longstanding dependence on bank financing for its long-term investment needs.

Average Trading Volume: 10,718,479

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: Yen30.85B

See more data about 3315 stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: NZ Super Fund Adjusts Substantial Stake in KMD Brands After Equity Raise
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from KMD Brands Limited ( (NZ:KMD) ).

New Zealand Superannuation Fund Nominees Limited, as nominee for the state-owned New Zealand Superannuation Fund, has disclosed an updated substantial holding in KMD Brands, now owning 201,502,056 ordinary shares, or 11.198% of the company. The change reflects both on-market sales and the allotment of new shares under KMD’s recent retail entitlement offer, slightly reducing NZSF’s percentage stake but maintaining it as a major long-term institutional shareholder, a signal of continued sovereign fund backing that remains relevant for other investors monitoring KMD’s register.

Between 14 and 28 April 2026, the fund sold 3,554,638 KMD shares on market while simultaneously participating in the retail shortfall bookbuild, acquiring 5,049,686 new shares as part of KMD’s capital-raising programme. The resulting mix of secondary sales and primary issuance has modestly diluted NZSF’s proportionate holding due to an enlarged share base, underscoring the impact of KMD’s equity raising on shareholder positions and the company’s evolving ownership structure.

The most recent analyst rating on (NZ:KMD) stock is a Buy with a N$0.14 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on KMD Brands Limited stock, see the NZ:KMD Stock Forecast page.

More about KMD Brands Limited

KMD Brands Limited is a outdoor apparel and equipment company best known for its Kathmandu, Rip Curl and Oboz brands, targeting adventure and lifestyle consumers in New Zealand, Australia and global markets. The group focuses on performance clothing, surfwear and footwear, serving both retail and wholesale channels and positioning itself as a specialist in outdoor and travel gear.

Average Trading Volume: 1,104,503

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: N$102.9M

data.description:

Invictus Energy Limited ( (AU:IVZ) ) has provided an announcement.

Invictus Energy has issued 4,166,667 new shares as payment for services in lieu of fees and a further 136 shares following the conversion of listed options, expanding its capital base without raising cash. The company confirmed the shares were issued without a prospectus under Australian Corporations Act provisions and stated it remains compliant with continuous disclosure and financial reporting obligations, with no undisclosed price-sensitive information identified for investors.

This technical cleansing notice assures the market that Invictus’ new securities can trade freely while affirming transparency and regulatory compliance around its capital management. The move underscores the company’s reliance on equity-based consideration to support its ongoing high-impact exploration activities in Zimbabwe’s Cabora Bassa Basin.

More about Invictus Energy Limited

Invictus Energy Ltd is an independent upstream oil and gas exploration company focused on high-impact energy resources in sub-Saharan Africa, particularly within Zimbabwe’s Cabora Bassa Basin. The Perth-headquartered, ASX-listed company has made a significant gas discovery at the Mukuyu field and continues to mature this frontier onshore African basin through ongoing exploration activities.

data.title: Critical Resources widens solid-state battery push with NTU thermal management licence
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Critical Resources Ltd ( (AU:CRR) ).

Critical Resources has secured an evaluation licence from NTUitive, the commercial arm of Nanyang Technological University in Singapore, for a portfolio of thermal management technologies covering lithium-ion batteries and high‑density electronics in data centres. The deal extends the company’s solid-state battery evaluation programme, linking its upstream lithium supply from Mavis Lake with downstream battery development and advanced cooling solutions.

The NTU technology uses a two-phase spray cooling system with a dielectric fluid that evaporates on contact with hot components, delivering significantly higher cooling efficiency and lower energy use than conventional air‑cooled systems. Validated research results show sharp cuts in facility energy consumption and chip temperatures, and the inclusion of model predictive control offers a potential pathway to smarter battery and electronics thermal management in hot climates, improving Critical Resources’ positioning in both energy storage and data-centre infrastructure markets.

More about Critical Resources Ltd

Critical Resources Limited is an ASX-listed company focused on the lithium value chain, leveraging its Mavis Lake Lithium Project to support downstream development of next-generation solid-state lithium-ion batteries. The company is expanding beyond raw material supply into battery technology innovation, targeting applications that demand reliable performance in elevated-temperature environments.

YTD Price Performance: -9.09%

Average Trading Volume: 4,445,556

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: A$25.55M

data.title: Landis+Gyr Sells OT Cybersecurity Subsidiary Rhebo to Everfield
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Landis+Gyr Group AG ( (CH:LAND) ) has shared an announcement.

Landis+Gyr Group AG has agreed to sell its subsidiary Rhebo GmbH, a Leipzig-based provider of security monitoring and anomaly detection for industrial OT networks and IIoT environments, to Everfield Germany GmbH, an investor focused on European B2B SaaS firms. The deal, valuing Rhebo in the high single-digit million US dollar range and pending regulatory approval, aligns with Landis+Gyr’s strategic refocus on core priorities while shifting Rhebo to an owner committed to further developing its OT security technology.

Management at Landis+Gyr frames the divestment as a move to sharpen its core business, while Everfield highlights Rhebo’s strong DACH market position and technical expertise as a valuable addition to its software ecosystem. Under Everfield’s long-term ownership model, Rhebo is expected to accelerate growth, enhance its cyber security offerings and deliver increased value to industrial customers across the EMEA region, potentially strengthening both companies’ positions in their respective markets.

The most recent analyst rating on (CH:LAND) stock is a Hold with a CHF59.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Landis+Gyr Group AG stock, see the CH:LAND Stock Forecast page.

More about Landis+Gyr Group AG

Landis+Gyr Group AG is a global leader in energy technologies and integrated energy management solutions for electricity, gas and water utilities. The company develops intelligent, connected devices and software that provide real-time grid transparency and system control, helping more than 3,500 utilities worldwide forecast demand and optimize operations for more reliable and sustainable energy delivery.

Average Trading Volume: 69,192

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

data.title: Renascor Starts Commissioning of Government-Backed Graphite Demonstration Plant
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Renascor Resources Limited ( (AU:RNU) ) has shared an update.

Renascor Resources has completed construction of its Australian Government co‑funded purified spherical graphite demonstration facility in Adelaide and has begun commissioning, keeping the project on schedule and within budget. The plant will process graphite from the Siviour deposit into battery‑grade material and customer samples, with commissioning progressing without lost‑time injuries.

The company is also advancing upstream project work, including optimisation of groundwater, tailings and mining studies, along with site enabling and accommodation planning. Exploration across its South Australian portfolio at Bulloo Creek, Marree, Wooltana, Tumby Bay and Olary continued during the quarter, supported by a strong cash position of about A$95 million as at 31 March 2026.

More about Renascor Resources Limited

Renascor Resources Limited is an Australian resources company focused on developing graphite assets in South Australia. Its primary products are purified spherical graphite for battery applications, sourced from the Siviour Graphite Deposit, with a market focus on supplying battery-grade materials to the global electric vehicle and energy storage sectors.

Average Trading Volume: 2,427,757

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Atomos ( (AU:AMS) ) has issued an announcement.

Atomos has issued 5,602,592 fully paid ordinary shares at $0.0175 each on 24 April 2026 and has confirmed the shares were issued without a prospectus under the relevant disclosure relief provisions. The company stated it is compliant with its financial reporting and continuous disclosure obligations and that there is no excluded information, positioning the new shares to trade freely while signaling ongoing regulatory adherence to investors.

The announcement, authorised by the board, underlines Atomos’ effort to access additional equity capital while maintaining transparency for stakeholders. By confirming there is no undisclosed price-sensitive information, the company aims to support market confidence and liquidity around the new share issuance within its global media technology franchise.

Atomos is a Melbourne-based technology company that designs hardware recorders, monitoring devices, software tools and cloud services for film and video production. data.title: Hallenstein Glasson Delivers Strong First-Half Profit Lift and Higher Dividend
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Hallenstein Glasson Holdings Limited ( (NZ:HLG) ) is now available.

Hallenstein Glasson Holdings reported a strong first half for the six months to 1 February 2026, with unaudited group sales rising 14.6% to $275.2 million and gross margin improving to 60.9% despite foreign exchange headwinds. Net profit after tax jumped 32.1% to $28.0 million, supported by robust performances in Glassons Australia, New Zealand operations, and the Hallensteins chain, alongside double-digit online sales growth.

The company is investing in store refurbishments, selective network expansion, and a new automated warehouse in Sydney to support future growth, while maintaining a strong balance sheet and tight inventory control. Directors have lifted the interim dividend to 29.0 cents per share, but caution that recent trading strength, aided by new and refurbished stores and favourable comparatives, may be difficult to sustain amid geopolitical tensions, rising living costs, and other macroeconomic pressures that could affect consumer demand and operating costs.

More about Hallenstein Glasson Holdings Limited

Hallenstein Glasson Holdings Limited is a New Zealand-based specialty apparel retailer operating the Glassons and Hallensteins brands across New Zealand and Australia. The group focuses on fashion clothing and related products, selling through a combined network of physical stores and a growing e-commerce channel that now contributes over 18% of total sales.

Average Trading Volume: 24,491

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: N$581.6M

data.title: Bank of Guizhou Highlights Strong Capital Ratios but Warns Q1 Data Are Unaudited
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Bank of Guizhou Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6199) ) has provided an update.

Bank of Guizhou Co., Ltd., a Guiyang-based commercial bank serving the mainland Chinese market, has reiterated that it operates under PRC regulation and is not licensed as a banking institution in Hong Kong. The bank remains subject to China Accounting Standards for Business Enterprises and stresses that its disclosures may differ from audited figures once formal reviews are completed.

The bank released supplemental first-quarter 2026 financial data, highlighting a capital adequacy ratio of 13.54%, a tier-one capital adequacy ratio of 11.67% and a core tier-one capital adequacy ratio of 10.84%, all above relevant regulatory thresholds. Management cautioned investors that the figures are unaudited, may be adjusted in the course of auditing and should not be the sole basis for investment decisions in the bank’s securities.

More about Bank of Guizhou Co. Ltd. Class H

Bank of Guizhou Co., Ltd. is a mainland Chinese commercial bank based in Guiyang, focusing on providing banking and financial services within the People’s Republic of China. Although listed in Hong Kong, it is not an authorized institution under Hong Kong’s Banking Ordinance and is not supervised by the Hong Kong Monetary Authority, nor permitted to conduct banking or deposit-taking business in Hong Kong.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

data.title: Kaili Resources Highlights South Australia Rare Earth Focus in March Quarter
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Kaili Resources Limited ( (AU:KLR) ) has provided an update.

Kaili Resources reported that its main exploration activities for the March 2026 quarter centred on rare earth element prospects in South Australia’s Limestone Coast, where it holds the Lameroo, Karte and Coodalya tenements over 1,989 square kilometres in the Murray Basin. The areas target ionic clay–style REE mineralisation within Loxton and Parilla sands, located about 200 kilometres east of Adelaide and close to existing infrastructure, and the company is also actively seeking copper, gold, silver and base metals projects to broaden its asset base.

The update places Kaili’s projects near Australian Rare Earths’ Koppamurra deposit, which already hosts a substantial JORC resource and is undergoing pre-feasibility studies, underscoring the regional prospectivity for REEs and highlighting potential strategic value if Kaili’s exploration proves successful. This geographic clustering in an emerging REE district may enhance Kaili’s positioning in the critical minerals supply chain and could improve future development options and investor interest, subject to exploration outcomes.

More about Kaili Resources Limited

Kaili Resources Limited is an Australian exploration company focused primarily on rare earth elements in South Australia, a sector benefiting from strong government support due to REEs’ critical role in semiconductors, magnets and defence applications. Through its wholly owned subsidiary Kaili Gold Pty Ltd, the company holds 100% interests in three tenements totaling 1,989 square kilometres in the Limestone Coast’s Murray Basin, and is also seeking copper, gold, silver and base metals projects to diversify its resources portfolio.

Average Trading Volume: 70,254

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: A$13.86M
